flag_description: Permafrost was not reached within the reported probe profile or observation limit.
Permafrost was confirmed but exact thaw or permafrost depth is only bounded.
Observation limit was assigned from protocol source interpretation or processor convention rather than a row-specific measured limit.
Permafrost absence is interpreted to the bottom of an observed soil or profile record.
Date was assigned from year campaign midpoint or representative thaw-season convention.
Source flags the observation date as approximate range-based or otherwise imprecise.
Site or event coordinate is repeated for observations that are more spatially granular than the coordinate.
Coordinates were assigned from lookup tables or interpolated along transects.
Source flags coordinate uncertainty likely coordinate problems or georeferenced coordinates.
Canonical observation value is a mean annual site summary or other source or processor summary statistic.
Permafrost state or depth was inferred from temperature profile logic.
Depth was inferred from GPR ERT or other geophysical interpretation rather than direct probing or coring.
Source or CUSP processor uses modeled estimated extrapolated or reconstructed depth.
Value or coordinate was extracted georeferenced or digitized from a figure.
Permafrost presence state was assigned from study context threshold logic or source convention rather than explicit row-level presence or absence.
Observation method is unknown or mapped approximately into the CUSP method vocabulary.
Units sentinels coordinate signs or source codes were recoded to make the row usable.
Known potential overlap with another source remains or source-specific deduplication affected interpretation.
Field notes indicate rock gravel obstruction bottoming out road water or similar ambiguity.
